subtitle: Agile Coach · System Integrations & Migrations · Scaled Delivery
hero_title:
I grow it.
Now I want to deliver it.
hero_sub: Three years as a home grower. Ten years as a Scrum Master. The passion for the plant is not borrowed from a job posting — and the delivery discipline has been tested at scale across enterprise, startup, and regulated environments.

the_case.need: 5+ years SM with multiple cross-functional development teams
the_case.answer: 10+ years — Eptura (~10 distributed teams, enterprise SaaS), A&E (~10 concurrent SVOD workstreams), Understood.org, Crocs, Stealth AI, DigitasLBI (SAFe), Paperless Post (NEXUS).
the_case.detail: The five-year ask is exceeded by double across every team type — enterprise SaaS, media/streaming, healthcare-adjacent, AI/data, financial services, and consumer e-commerce. Each engagement is a different domain, a different team composition, and a different technical context. The consistent outcomes are the same: ceremonies running cleanly, backlogs that are ready to build, blockers surfacing before they stall a sprint, and delivery metrics that inform decisions rather than decorate dashboards.

the_case.need: Strong experience supporting system integrations and migration projects
the_case.answer: Sitecore→Contentful CMS migration at Understood.org. Cross-environment integration release governance at Crocs. WooCommerce/Stripe/Printify API integrations at OVRSTCH. AI data pipeline delivery at Stealth AI. Google Gemini API integration via Creative Coder.
the_case.detail: System integrations and migrations are the deliverables I have the most specific experience protecting. At Understood.org I led SM delivery for a complex CMS migration — requirements through go-live, coordinating Engineering, QA, Content, and Compliance, with no critical regressions at launch. At Crocs I managed cross-environment integration dependencies through the full build→test→UAT→cutover lifecycle under a hard go-live deadline. Integration delivery is where the cross-team dependency management and release sequencing discipline matters most.

the_case.need: Azure DevOps and/or Jira — hands-on, backlog and sprint management
the_case.answer: ADO configuration owner at Eptura PMO (~10 teams). Jira admin-level at A&E (built from scratch), Understood.org (Service Desk), Crocs (SOD compliance workflow configuration), and Eptura. Jira AI embedded at Crocs — ~30% overhead reduction.
the_case.detail: Both tools at administration level — not just daily use. At A&E I took teams from Post-it notes to a fully administered Jira instance across ~10 workstreams. At Eptura I was the ADO configuration owner for the full PMO and built the Smartsheet dashboards that synthesized delivery health for senior leadership. At Crocs I configured Jira SOD compliance controls to meet SOX audit requirements. The tools are not the skill — knowing what to build in Jira or ADO so the visibility it creates is actually useful to the people who need it is the skill.

the_case.need: Scaled Agile frameworks — SAFe, LeSS, or NEXUS
the_case.answer: SAFe at DigitasLBI/SunTrust (PI planning, ART coordination, offshore delivery). NEXUS at Paperless Post. Scrum-of-Scrums at scale at Eptura (~10 teams) and A&E (~10 concurrent workstreams).
the_case.detail: SAFe in a live SAFe enterprise environment (DigitasLBI/SunTrust, 2014) — PI planning, ART-aligned delivery across onshore and offshore teams. NEXUS coaching and training at Paperless Post, developed for two concurrent teams. Scrum-of-Scrums as a sustained operational practice at Eptura and A&E across ~10 teams simultaneously. The framework is the structure — the underlying scaled delivery discipline is consistent across all of them.

the_case.need: Technical background — software engineering concepts, DevOps familiarity
the_case.answer: Former front-end engineer (Organic Inc., Flavorpill). Current: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Three.js, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Google Gemini API — six production applications shipped in 2026. Can read code, review a deployment pipeline, and hold a technical conversation with DevOps without losing fidelity.
the_case.detail: The technical foundation is not borrowed from proximity to engineers — I hand-coded HTML, JSP, PHP, JavaScript, and CSS for Bloomingdale's e-commerce at my first job out of college. This year I shipped six production web applications using Claude Code as the AI coding agent — audio DSP, real-time 3D, game physics, and a Next.js portfolio with a full security-hardening pass. CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions. When I coordinate with a DevOps team on a release, I understand what they are actually dealing with.

the_case.answer: Certified Scrum Master (CSM) — Scrum Alliance, current.
the_case.detail: CSM is the required cert and it is held current. Also have practical SAFe delivery experience from DigitasLBI/SunTrust and NEXUS coaching experience from Paperless Post — the theory is grounded in real delivery practice at scale.

the_stack.headline: The delivery toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: The platforms and practices behind ten years of SM delivery.

nodes.label: Jira & Azure DevOps
nodes.desc: Admin-level fluency — workflows, boards, dashboards, SOD compliance controls, sprint health, velocity, and portfolio reporting
nodes.pm_role: Built Jira from scratch at A&E. Configured ADO at Eptura PMO. Jira SOD compliance controls at Crocs. Jira AI embedded for delivery automation. Both tools at the administration level.

nodes.label: Jira AI & Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted backlog management, sprint summaries, and delivery reporting automation
nodes.pm_role: Embedded at Crocs — ~30% administrative overhead reduction. Output quality improved because I had time to review instead of generate. AI handles the recurring output; human judgment owns every result.

nodes.label: GitHub & CI/CD
nodes.desc: Git version control, GitHub Actions CI/CD, deployment pipeline coordination
nodes.pm_role: Owned CI/CD pipelines for six production applications via GitHub Actions. Coordinated build→test→UAT→cutover at Crocs. Can read a pipeline and coordinate a release without losing anything in translation.

nodes.label: Scaled Frameworks
nodes.desc: SAFe (PI planning, ART coordination), NEXUS (multi-team Scrum), Scrum-of-Scrums at scale
nodes.pm_role: SAFe in a live SAFe enterprise at DigitasLBI/SunTrust. NEXUS coaching at Paperless Post. Scrum-of-Scrums across ~10 teams at Eptura and A&E. Framework-agnostic — the delivery discipline is consistent.

nodes.label: Integration & Migration Delivery
nodes.desc: CMS migrations, API integrations, cross-environment release sequencing, and data pipeline delivery
nodes.pm_role: Sitecore→Contentful at Understood.org. Cross-environment release governance at Crocs. WooCommerce/Stripe/Printify at OVRSTCH. AI data pipelines at Stealth AI. Integration delivery is the specialty.

when_things_break.scenario: A cross-team integration dependency surfaces mid-sprint — two teams are blocked on each other
response: Convene both team leads immediately — not at the next ceremony
Map the exact dependency: what does Team A need from Team B, by when, and what is the sprint impact?
Facilitate a resolution with the right decision-makers in the room — not a relay of messages
Add the dependency to the RAID log with an owner, a resolution date, and a communication plan
Build a lightweight synchronization cadence between the two teams for the rest of the sprint

when_things_break.scenario: A migration go-live is at risk — QA found a data integrity issue two days before cutover
response: Stop the cutover clock — announce a hold before anyone is surprised by it
Get the scope of the data issue in writing from QA before any engineering work starts on the fix
Escalate to leadership immediately with a concrete decision request: delay, de-scope, or accept documented risk
Document the decision and the owner before the engineering team resumes
Add a data integrity checkpoint to the Definition of Done for every future migration sprint

when_things_break.scenario: A team is resisting retro action items — they happen but nothing changes
response: Acknowledge it directly in the next retro: 'Action items from last three retros — what happened?'
Stop generating new action items until the old ones are closed or explicitly abandoned with a reason
Reduce to one action item per retro — owned by a specific person, with a specific done criterion
Make the action item the first agenda item of the next retro, not an afterthought
If the resistance is cultural and systemic, escalate with data: here is what the pattern shows

how_i_run_a_room: Every ceremony has a stated outcome — if it doesn't, it doesn't happen
Integration dependencies get a named owner and a resolution date within 24 hours of surfacing
Migration go-live decisions are made with the right people in the room — not ratified after the fact
Retro action items are the first agenda item of the next retro — not archived notes
Blockers surface at standup with a proposed path forward — not at retrospective as a postmortem
Jira AI handles recurring reporting — human review before it leaves the building
The team owns their process — the SM creates the conditions, not the dependency

track_record.role: Digital Producer & Scrum Master — CMS Migration
points: Led SM delivery for Sitecore→Contentful migration — requirements through go-live
Coordinated Engineering, QA, Content, and Compliance across a 12+ country team
On-time launch, no critical regressions — took over a distressed program in its final months
WCAG 2.1 AA enforced as a non-negotiable Definition of Done throughout the migration
Managed cross-environment integration dependencies through build→test→UAT→cutover
SOX-compliant release governance with Go/No-Go cadence and CAB-style change approval
Jira AI and Copilot embedded — ~30% administrative overhead reduction
On-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization under hard deadline pressure

track_record.role: Agile Coach & Senior Scrum Master — Enterprise PMO
points: ~10 distributed teams (India and California) — ADO configuration owner, Jira standardization
35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction
Scrum-of-Scrums, program-level planning, and scaled delivery coordination at PMO level
Mentored junior SMs; facilitated org-wide Agile mentorship across the enterprise
~10 concurrent SVOD workstreams — Web, iOS, tvOS, Android, Roku — Scrum-of-Scrums
Built Jira from scratch across all teams; two-year cultural transformation
Shipped all platforms against externally committed, publicly visible launch windows

before: Cross-team integration dependencies surface at the sprint review — too late to protect the delivery
Migration go-live decisions get made by whoever is in the room when things go sideways
Retro action items accumulate — they are documented but nothing actually changes
Blocked teams wait for the next standup to surface what they needed resolved yesterday
after: Integration dependencies have owners and resolution dates within 24 hours — tracked in RAID
Migration go-live decisions are made with the right people in the room and documented before work resumes
Retro action items are the first agenda item at the next retro — owned, done-criteriaed, closed
Blockers surface at standup with a proposed path forward — momentum is protected, not recovered

the_ramp.deliverable: Meet the teams, the POs, and the engineering leads. Audit current Jira/ADO setup, ceremony cadence, and any active integration or migration workstreams. Understand what 'done' actually means for Curaleaf's development teams.
Facilitate first full ceremony cycle. Identify the top 3 cross-team dependency risks that aren't currently tracked. Propose the first process improvement based on what the metrics show.
RAID log live for active integration workstreams. Jira workflows and sprint reporting running cleanly. First retro action item implemented and confirmed done — not just committed.

Teams running ceremonies independently with measurable improvement in delivery predictability. Integration dependency risks surfacing in planning rather than at cutover. Leadership has real-time delivery visibility without asking.
